info.title: Podhome Integration API
info.description:
### Overview

The Podhome Integration API lets you automate your podcast workflow programmatically.

**Episodes**
- Create, update, schedule, publish and delete episodes
- Upload audio files via presigned URLs
- Run Podhome AI (transcripts, chapters) and Audio Enhancement
- Get episode details, processing status and download counts

**Chapters, Clips & People**
- Add, update and remove chapters from episodes
- Create audio clips (soundbites) for promotion
- Manage people and guests across your show

**Transcripts**
- Retrieve episode transcripts (with optional SRT format)

**Analytics**
- Get download counts, unique listeners, and follower metrics
- Break down by podcast app, country, device, OS, and episode
- View time-series trends for growth analysis

**Webhooks**
- Register, list and delete webhook subscriptions
- Get notified when episodes are published or live sessions start

**Show Info**
- Retrieve show details and settings

**AI Assistants (MCP)**
- All of the above is also available through our [MCP server](https://www.podhome.fm/docs/mcp), which lets AI assistants like Claude manage your podcast through natural conversation.

### Authentication

Pass your show API key in the `X-API-KEY` header. Find your key in show settings under Integration.

Questions? support@podhome.fm

get.description:
Retrieve analytics data for your show or a specific episode.
            
**Overview** (no dimension or metric): Returns total downloads, unique listeners, and top episodes.
            
**Breakdown** (specify dimension): Returns data broken down by apps, countries, devices, os, time_of_day, or episodes.
            
**Trends** (specify metric): Returns time-series data for downloads or audience (unique listeners) over days, weeks, or months.

parameters.name: episode_id
parameters.description: Optional episode ID (GUID) to filter to a specific episode.

parameters.name: dimension
parameters.description: Breakdown dimension: apps, countries, devices, os, time_of_day, or episodes.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Trend granularity: days, weeks, or months. Default: days

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional status filter. Allowed values: 0 = Draft, 1 = Scheduled, 2 = Published, 3 = LivePending, 4 = Live, 5 = LiveEnded

parameters.name: includeTranscript
parameters.description: Optional. When true the response will include one transcript per episode (prefer SRT if available).

parameters.name: includeChapters
parameters.description: Optional. When true the response will include chapters for each episode.

parameters.name: includeDownloads
parameters.description: Optional. When true the response will include the all-time downloads for each episode.

parameters.name: includePeople
parameters.description: Optional. When true the response will include people each episode.

example.title: Inside Evergreen Podcasts: A Conversation with David Allen Moss
example.description:
Welcome to another episode of About Podcasting. This week, I had the pleasure of speaking with David Allen Moss from Evergreen Podcasts. Evergreen is a premier podcast network with over 350 shows, and David shared insights into what a podcast network actually is, what they do for podcasters, and how they operate. We delved into the history of Evergreen, from its beginnings as Front Porch People to its current status, and discussed the types of content they produce, including originals, branded content, and partner shows.

David explained the passion behind Evergreen, emphasizing their love for storytelling and the diverse backgrounds of their team members, which include writers, creative directors, and musicians. We also touched on the importance of community in podcasting and how Evergreen fosters a collaborative and supportive environment.

We explored the services Evergreen provides to podcasters, such as hosting, analytics, ad management, and marketing support. David highlighted the custom relationships they build with their partners, helping them grow and monetize their shows.

Additionally, we discussed the evolving landscape of podcasting, including the rise of podcasting 2.0 and the importance of staying nimble and innovative. David shared Evergreen's future plans, including potential global expansion and continued growth in sales and partnerships.

For more information about Evergreen Podcasts, visit evergreenpodcasts.com or find them on social media at @streamevergreen. You can also connect with David Allen Moss on LinkedIn.

example.title: The Essentials of Podcasting: Directories, Apps, and Hosts

people.name: Nick
people.description: Nick is a podcast enthusiast and host of About Podcasting. With a passion for storytelling and audio content, Nick brings insightful conversations and expert advice to aspiring podcasters.

post.description: Permanently deletes an episode. This cannot be undone. If the episode was published, it will be removed from the RSS feed.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

get.description:
Returns a single episode with full details including chapters, clips, processing status and download count.
Useful for polling the status of a newly created episode.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

post.description:
Returns a presigned upload URL. Upload the file via HTTP PUT to the returned URL, then call finalize_upload.
The upload URL is valid for 2 hours. The file is uploaded directly to storage — it does not pass through the Podhome API.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

post.description:
Call this after you have successfully uploaded the file to the presigned URL returned by begin_upload.
This triggers audio processing, transcription, and other configured features.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

post.description: Adds a person (host, guest, etc.) to an episode. If a person with the same name already exists they will be reused.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

post.description: Removes a person from an episode. The person record itself is not deleted — only the link to this episode is removed.

400.description: API key missing or validation error.

get.description: Returns basic metadata about the show associated with the API key.

example.title: About Podcasting

example.author: Barry Luijbregts

400.description: API key missing.

post.description:
Creates a new podcast show under the same account as the show associated with your API key.
You must provide a show image — either as a publicly accessible URL (image_url) or base64-encoded data (image_data).
The image must be square and at least 1400x1400 pixels (max 3000x3000). JPG, PNG, or GIF.

400.description: Validation error or missing required fields.

get.description: Returns the transcript content for an episode. Returns the best available format: SRT preferred, then VTT, then plain text.

parameters.name: episode_id
parameters.description: The episode Id to get the transcript for.

example.content:
1
00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:05,000
Welcome to the show!

2
00:00:05,000 --> 00:00:10,000
Today we are going to talk about podcasting.

use_podhome_ai.description: Indicates if you want Podhome AI to run. When true, Podhome AI generates a transcript and optionally other artifacts

suggest_chapters.description: Indicates if you want Podhome AI to generate Chapters for your episode. Only works when use_podhome_ai = true.

suggest_details.description: Indicates if you want Podhome AI to generate a Description and Title Suggestions for your episode. Only works when use_podhome_ai = true.

suggest_clips.description: Indicates if you want Podhome AI to generate clips (SoundBites) for your episode. Only works when use_podhome_ai = true.

enhance_audio.description: Indicates if you want to run Audio Enhancement on this episode. Note that Audio Enhancement is a paid feature. Costs are incurred per processed minute of audio

chapter_id.description: Id of the chapter to modify.

episode_id.description: Id of the episode the chapter belongs to.

title.description: Optional new title for the chapter.

start_time.description: Optional new start time in seconds (can be fractional).

description.description: Optional new description for the chapter.

image_url.description: Optional new image URL for the chapter. Empty string clears the image.

url.description: Optional new URL associated with the chapter. Empty string clears the URL.

chapter_id.description: The Id of the modified chapter.

clip_id.description: Id of the clip to modify.

episode_id.description: Id of the episode the clip belongs to.

title.description: Optional new title for the clip.

start_time.description: Optional new start time in seconds (can be fractional).

duration.description: Optional new duration in seconds (can be fractional).

clip_id.description: The Id of the modified clip.

episode_id.description: Id of the episode to modify.

title.description: Optional new title for the episode (max 2048 characters).

description.description: Optional new description (can contain HTML like paragraphs and breaks).

episode_nr.description: Optional episode number (must be greater than 0).

season_nr.description: Optional season number (must be greater than 0).

image_url.description: Optional image URL (publicly accessible HTTP/HTTPS URL). Empty string clears the image.

image_data.description: Optional base64 encoded image data. Supports data URL format (e.g., "data:image/png;base64,...") or raw base64 string.

alternate_enclosure_url.description: Optional alternate enclosure URL (e.g. a video version). Empty string clears the alternate enclosure. MIME type is auto-detected from extension if alternate_enclosure_type is omitted.

alternate_enclosure_type.description: Optional MIME type for the alternate enclosure (e.g. "video/mp4", "audio/aac"). Auto-detected from URL extension if omitted.

episode_id.description: The Id of the modified episode.

episode_nr.description: Updated episode number (null if not set).

season_nr.description: Updated season number (null if not set).

image_url.description: Updated image URL (null if not set).

alternate_enclosure_url.description: Alternate enclosure URL (null if not set).

alternate_enclosure_type.description: Alternate enclosure MIME type (null if not set).

image.description: Url of the person image. Can be an empty string

url.description: A Public URL to the person's website or social profile

role.description: Role of the person, like guest, host and others

episode_id.description: Id of the episode to remove the person from.

person_id.description: Id of the person to remove from the episode.

publish_date.description: Future UTC ISO-8601 publish date. Ignored if publish_now = true.

status.description:
Status of the episode. Possible values:         
Draft = 0,
Scheduled = 1,
Published = 2,
LivePending = 3,
Live = 4,
LiveEnded = 5,

action_type.description:
Possible values for action_type:
            "episode_published" (register webhook for when episode is published)
            "episode_live" (register webhook for when episode live session starts)

id.description: The Id of the created Webhook. Use the id to test or remove your webhook registration

integration_id.description: The Id of the Webhook. Optional, if not provided all webhooks will be tested.

transcript_url.description: Public URL to the transcript file

action_type.description:
possible values for action_type:
           "episode_published" (webhook for when episode is published)
           "episode_live" (webhook for when episode live session starts)

WebhookSubscriptionDto.description: An array of active webhook subscription.

ApiKey.description: Provide your show API key in the X-API-KEY header.
ApiKey.name: X-API-KEY

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Manage webhook subscriptions to receive real-time notifications when episodes are published or live sessions start. Webhooks allow you to integrate Podhome with your own systems and automate workflows. Note that our requests to your webhook time out after 60 seconds.

tags.name: Episodes
tags.description: Create, schedule, and manage podcast episodes programmatically. Upload audio files, set metadata, and publish episodes directly through the API.

tags.name: Clips (SoundBites)
tags.description: Create audio clips (soundbites) from your episodes. Clips are short segments that can be used for promotion and social media sharing.

tags.name: Shows
tags.description: Create new podcast shows. Provide show metadata including title, description, artwork image, language, and Apple Podcasts category. The new show is created under the same account as the show associated with your API key.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Retrieve podcast analytics data including download counts, unique listeners, and breakdowns by podcast app, country, device, operating system, time of day, and episode. Supports time-series trends for growth analysis.
